rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for interface assumptions/guarantees, and implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model; do not introduce package-qualified names or undefined references.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture structure during AGREE fusion by placing contracts in appropriate scopes.
Do not introduce new data types, ports, or properties that are not present in the input AADL model.
Ensure all AGREE expressions reference identifiers that are explicitly declared or visible in the current component scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for interface-level assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for subcomponent bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes or vice versa.
Do not reference undefined or package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
